evaluationFactors: Technical Approach (50 points) - Assesses the extent to which the proposed System and technical approach will result in high quality services and deliverables meeting OGC objectives and requirements; systems meeting all requirements with little or no customization rated more highly

Experience and Qualifications (25 points) - Evaluates breadth of experience with recent projects of similar scope, size, and complexity; greater consideration given to contracts most relevant to the RFP including government contracts

Past Performance and Customer Service (25 points) - Assesses consistent demonstration of commitment to customer satisfaction and timely delivery of high-quality products and services; focuses on projects similar in scope, size, and complexity; greater consideration given to relevant government contracts
Price - Evaluated for reasonableness, completeness, and price realism; not scored; non-price factors when combined are approximately equal to price
overview: The District of Columbia Courts (D.C. Courts), on behalf of the Office of the General Counsel (OGC), is seeking a qualified vendor to provide a cloud-hosted, configurable commercial-off-the-shelf (COTS) Legal Case and Document Management System under Solicitation DCSC-26-RFP-60. The business problem being addressed is the modernization of OGC's operations, which currently lack a legacy legal or document management system and rely on approximately 1.2 TB of confidential, privileged, and litigation-sensitive materials stored across 10,000 folders on an NTFS network drive comprising roughly 390,000 files. Success looks like a fully implemented system that provides electronic law practice management, document and case management, contract and eDiscovery management, generative AI capabilities, automated workflows, robust search, role-based security, Section 508 compliance, and seamless integration with the Courts' enterprise IT environment including Microsoft 365, Citrix VDI, Azure, and Adobe Acrobat Pro DC, with minimal customization required. Key constraints include that this is a firm-fixed-price contract with a base year and four option years; the system must be hosted by a FedRAMP-authorized Cloud Service Provider; must comply with NIST 800-53 r5/800-37 r2 security controls; all contractor personnel must obtain security clearances per DCCS policies; subcontractors outside the United States may not access Court documents or data; proposals are due by email no later than 11:00 a.m. EST on April 24, 2026; and the Courts anticipate awarding a single contract to the most advantageous offeror considering price and non-price factors that are approximately equal in weight. OGC currently has six staff members requiring licenses, with potential expansion to ten licenses in future option years. The procurement scope includes software licenses, warranties, installation, document migration of approximately 1.2 TB of files, implementation, on-site post-implementation support, end-user training, and ongoing maintenance and support.

requirements.requirement: Provide a cloud-hosted, configurable COTS Legal Case and Document Management System (not a Court Case Management System)
Document management with a centralized database, native document management, and searchable repository
Case management with streamlined, real-time matter intake, opening, closure, and matter type tracking
Litigation hold tracking with electronic reminders and ability to confirm receipt, e-sign, and record recipient response
Task and calendar management capabilities with automatic alerts for case tracking and monitoring
Contract management tool or function, including tracking and providing reminders of deadlines for procurements, contracts, and other agreements
Generative AI capability including automated workflow, intelligent scanning, data verification, data analysis, legal research, document review, audit trails, data encryption, and GDPR compliance

eDiscovery including comprehensive data source support, custodian management, data filtering and de-duplication, metadata extraction and analysis, secure storage and preservation, and automated review features (redaction and legal holds)
File sharing including securely sharing confidential legal files
Robust search capability including search by document profile fields and within document text in MS Word, Excel, PDF, HTML, and other widely-used file format types
Capture/import email, documents, attachments, transcripts, video or audio recordings (including voicemail) and other file types
Secure, role-based access to case/document profiles and documents (e.g., edit, view only, create or save as new version, delete)
Accessibility and mobility for easy access anywhere with a secured environment
OCR and redaction capability
Ability to generate template documents
Compatible with Citrix Virtual Desktop Infrastructure (VDI)
Multiple or unlimited number of document profile fields and categories, including a case notes field(s)
Single sign-on
Web-based solution compatible with the latest versions of Microsoft Edge, Chrome, Firefox, and Safari
Real time user response rate for searching, accessing, saving, and uploading and downloading files, including large files
Near real time response rate for generating reports
Disaster Recovery, including immediate access to backup files and documents
Section 508 compliance (29 U.S.C. §794d)
Mobile accessibility

Compatibility with MS Word, MS Excel, MS Outlook (including add-in for saving emails, attachments, and contacts), MS Outlook Calendar with alerts and reminders, MS SharePoint, Adobe Acrobat Pro DC, Citrix ShareFile, and Box.com
As-built documentation
Provide cloud-managed services including migration, configuration, and integration with DCCS enterprise IT environment
Migrate approximately 1.2 TB of OGC files (approximately 390,000 files across 10,000 folders) from NTFS network drive
Provide minimum 6 software licenses initially, with ability to scale to 10 licenses in future option years
Security features to prevent Contractor and subcontractor staff from accessing, copying, or downloading sensitive and confidential files, documents, profiles, records, and other information
Comply with NIST 800-53 r5/800-37 r2 security controls
FedRAMP authorized Cloud Service Provider
Require user authentication using single sign-on with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) or Microsoft Active Directory
Support configuration of rules for user passwords including frequency of changes, length, and character requirements
Lock user accounts after a defined number of failed password attempts
Secure user information through password encryption
Allow assignment of roles and permissions to users and groups of users
Assign users and user groups permissions to access confidential data and documents
Allow users to be assigned to multiple roles and resolve access to data accordingly
Define permission levels for access to all system components
Assign confidentiality to case data and documents
Prohibit reports containing full social security numbers (SSN) from being generated without SSNs being masked
Encrypt all data at rest and in transit
Ensure system has MFA (Multi-Factor Authentication) enabled
All contractor personnel must obtain security clearance as designated by the Contracting Officer in accordance with DCCS security policies
Subcontractors located or performing services outside the United States shall not have access to Court documents and/or data
SAM registration required prior to contract award
Submit invoices electronically through U.S. Department of the Treasury's Invoice Processing Platform (IPP)
Maintain drug-free workplace certification per Attachment J.7
Execute Anti-Collusion Statement (Attachment J.2)
Execute Ethics in Public Contracting acknowledgment (Attachment J.3)
Execute Non-Discrimination certification (Attachment J.4)
Execute Certification of Eligibility (Attachment J.5)
Submit Tax Certification Affidavit (Attachment J.6)

Provide certificates of insurance prior to commencing work: Commercial General Liability ($1M per occurrence/$2M aggregate), Automobile Liability ($1M per occurrence), Workers' Compensation per statutory mandates, and Employer's Liability ($500K)
Comply with Service Contract Act of 1965 and applicable wage determination (WD 2015-4281)
Obtain prior written approval from CO before making any public statement or publication bearing on work performed under the contract
Provide software licenses, documentation, and warranties
Provide installation, document migration, and implementation services
Provide on-site post-implementation support and end-user training
Provide ongoing maintenance and end-user support
Provide system documentation including electronic system administration manuals and application guides
Provide disaster recovery/business continuity plan
Provide training plan including web-based on-demand training materials and step-by-step procedure manuals with solution screenshots
Provide license and maintenance agreement, SLA, and/or SaaS agreement as applicable
Minimize personnel changes during performance of installation, document migration, implementation, post-implementation support, and user training
Only personnel stated in the proposal shall report for duty unless otherwise approved by the Courts

format: Proposals must be submitted electronically via email to grace.alao@dccsystem.gov
Email subject line must state: PROPOSAL FOR DCSC-26-RFP-60
Proposals must be typed in Times New Roman 12-point font size with 1-inch margins
Proposals must be submitted as two separate PDF files
Mailed, hand-delivered, telephonic, or faxed proposals will not be accepted
First page of each volume must be conspicuously marked with offeror name, address, solicitation number, caption, and due date/time
attachmentsAndForms: Attachment J.2 - Anti-Collusion Statement (executed and returned with proposal)
Attachment J.3 - Ethics in Public Contracting (signed)
Attachment J.5 - Certification of Eligibility (signed and notarized)
Attachment J.6 - Tax Certification Affidavit (sworn, for bids over $100,000)
Attachment J.7 - Certification Regarding a Drug-Free Workplace (signed)
Attachment J.8 - Past Performance Evaluation Form (completed for each referenced project/contract)
Attachment J.11 - DC Courts Oracle Supplier Request Form
Section A.1 - Solicitation, Offer and Award Form (signed)
Section K - Representations and Certifications (completed and signed)
Copy of license/permit/registration/certification to transact business in DC, or certification of intent to obtain
Copy of license and maintenance agreement, SLA, and/or SaaS agreement
Copy of disaster recovery/business continuity plan

requiredDocuments.name: Volume 1 - Technical Proposal
requiredDocuments.description: Technical proposal organized by tabs covering Contractor Information and Disclosures (Tab A), Technical Approach (Tab B), Experience and Qualifications (Tab C), and Past Performance and Customer Service (Tab D)
requiredDocuments.pageLimit: Tab B: 20 pages; Tab C: 5 pages

requiredDocuments.name: Transmittal Letter (Tab A)
requiredDocuments.description: Letter summarizing the Offeror's ability to supply requested products and services, containing a statement of willingness to provide services subject to RFP terms, signed by a legally authorized person

requiredDocuments.name: Past Performance and Customer Service (Tab D)
requiredDocuments.description: Past Performance Evaluation Forms (Attachment J.8) for each referenced project/contract (minimum 3, maximum 5 projects within last 3 years), plus disclosure of any terminated government contracts in last 5 years

requiredDocuments.name: Volume 2 - Price Proposal
requiredDocuments.description: Price proposal with fixed prices for each CLIN and performance period using the Price/Cost Schedule in Section B.2, plus assumptions, conditions, and exceptions
requiredDocuments.pageLimit: Tab A: 5 pages; Tab B: 5 pages

requiredDocuments.name: Certifications, Representations, and Attachments
requiredDocuments.description: Completed Section K Representations and Certifications, plus all executed Section J attachments including Anti-Collusion Statement (J.2), Ethics in Public Contracting (J.3), Non-Discrimination (J.4), Certification of Eligibility (J.5), Tax Certification Affidavit (J.6), Drug-Free Workplace Certification (J.7), and Oracle Supplier Request Form (J.11)

requiredVolumes: Volume 1 - Technical Proposal (Tab A: Contractor Information and Disclosures; Tab B: Technical Approach - 20 page limit; Tab C: Experience and Qualifications - 5 page limit; Tab D: Past Performance and Customer Service)
Volume 2 - Price Proposal (Tab A: Price/Cost Schedule - 5 page limit; Tab B: Assumptions, Conditions, and Exceptions - 5 page limit)

deliverables: On-site Post-Implementation Support and End User Training
Ongoing Maintenance and End User Support
System Documentation (electronic system administration manuals and application guides)
Training Plan and Training Materials (including web-based on-demand materials and step-by-step procedure manuals with screenshots)
License and Maintenance Agreement, SLA, and/or SaaS Agreement
